title: Mexican Burrito Bowl

subs.name: soya chunks or tofu for a vegetarian bowl — marinate and pan-fry the same way

ingredients.name: canned or cooked black beans, drained

subs.name: rajma (kidney beans) pressure-cooked and lightly seasoned is a natural Indian equivalent
roasted chickpeas add a similar bite and protein boost

ingredients.name: onion — half for chicken marinade, half for pico

subs.name: a spoonful of guacamole or simply thick curd with lime

steps.text: Mix 1 tsp red chilli powder, 1 tsp cumin, half the minced garlic, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tbsp oil and salt. Coat the chicken thoroughly and set aside. While the chicken marinates, cook the rice according to your usual method, then stir in a little lime juice and half the coriander.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a kadai or frying pan on high flame. Sear the marinated chicken for 5–6 minutes per side until charred at the edges and cooked through. Rest 3 minutes then slice into strips.

In the same pan over medium flame, add 1 tbsp oil and the remaining garlic. Add the drained black beans with the remaining chilli powder, cumin and salt. Stir and cook for 4–5 minutes until the beans are heated through and slightly saucy. Add a splash of water if they look dry.

Toast the corn in the same dry pan for 2–3 minutes until lightly charred — this caramelises the sugars and adds depth. Combine the diced tomato, remaining onion (diced), remaining coriander and the rest of the lime juice with a pinch of salt to make a quick pico.

Build each bowl: start with a generous scoop of coriander rice, then arrange the chicken strips, spiced beans, charred corn and pico in sections. Add sliced avocado and a dollop of curd if using. Serve immediately so the warm components contrast with the fresh pico.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → soya chunks or tofu for a vegetarian bowl — marinate and pan-fry the same way; black-beans → rajma (kidney beans) pressure-cooked and lightly seasoned is a natural Indian equivalent; avocado → a spoonful of guacamole or simply thick curd with lime; corn → roasted chickpeas add a similar bite and protein boost
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Rajma is the ideal substitute for black beans and needs no searching — pressure-cook 1 cup soaked rajma for 5–6 whistles, season with cumin and chilli powder. Use your regular kadai for the chicken and a pressure cooker for the beans. The entire bowl can be assembled with ingredients that are either in Indian pantries or easy to find in a metro city.
